vibe: Late-night Tokyo energy — deep house warmth, city pop shimmer, and left-field discoveries to close out Thursday

tracks.name: Tokai
tracks.reason: You've been deep in Tatsuro Yamashita territory — Taeko Onuki is his wife and this 1977 SUNSHOWER cut is peak city pop with Ryuichi Sakamoto on keys. If you don't know it yet, it'll feel like finding a secret level.

tracks.name: Sparkles
tracks.reason: French deep house with that same soulful warmth you get from Jimpster and &ME, but Folamour brings this jazzy, sunset-on-the-terrace energy that's hard to resist.

tracks.name: La cumbia me está llamando (Kaidi Tatham Remix)
tracks.reason: You vibed with Ezra Collective's danceable UK jazz — this is Nubya Garcia going full cumbia with Kaidi Tatham flipping it into broken-beat gold. London jazz scene at its most adventurous.

tracks.name: potage
tracks.reason: Japanese math-rock trio with insane time signatures that somehow still groove. Being in Tokyo right now, this is basically required listening — intricate guitar work that'll make your brain tingle.

tracks.name: Red Room
tracks.reason: Melbourne neo-soul that sits right between your Sade love and your Nujabes side. Nai Palm's vocals over that wobbly bass is pure ear candy — the kind of track that rewards headphones.

tracks.name: Solitude
tracks.reason: Japanese lo-fi jazz producer in the Nujabes lineage — warm piano loops and dusty drums. If Uyama Hiroto's 81Summer hit right, this is the perfect next step down that rabbit hole.

tracks.name: Falaise
tracks.reason: Sam Shepherd builds these intricate electronic textures that feel alive — sits in that same cerebral space as Overmono but more minimal and hypnotic. Late-night headphone territory.

tracks.name: Love Reigns
tracks.reason: Lo-fi house with raw, tape-saturated energy — if Peggy Gou's stuff hits for you, Mall Grab is the grittier, more underground cousin. This one's got that 3AM warehouse warmth.

tracks.name: MOJABI GHOST
tracks.reason: You had Central Cee's Gata on repeat — Tainy's production here takes reggaeton into experimental electronic territory with Bad Bunny. It's the weird, beautiful side of Latin music.

tracks.name: You Are In My System (Club Mix)
tracks.reason: Deep house royalty — Kerri Chandler is the bridge between your Moodymann love and the soulful house side. This club mix is pure groove, the kind of track DJs build entire sets around.
